title: OpenAI Launched GPT-5.5 For ChatGPT, Codex, And API Developers
summary: OpenAI announced GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro availability for ChatGPT and Codex paid/business plans, later updated the announcement to mark API availability, and listed API token pricing plus Batch, Flex, and Priority processing multipliers.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official announcement reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

impacts.recommended_action: Review plan eligibility, API allowlists, model routing, context-window assumptions, and regression coverage before moving production traffic to GPT-5.5.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: $5/1M input tokens and $30/1M output tokens for gpt-5.5; $30/1M input and $180/1M output for gpt-5.5-pro
estimated_cost_effect.basis: OpenAI announcement pricing section; Batch and Flex at half standard rate and Priority at 2.5x standard rate.

impacts.recommended_action: Update cost models and gateway pricing metadata before exposing GPT-5.5 or GPT-5.5 Pro through API defaults, agent routers, or self-service model selectors.

detail.migration_notes: The April 23 announcement covers ChatGPT and Codex rollout first, with an April 24 update for API availability. APW records public availability and listed API pricing; account eligibility and final API model IDs should be verified in the target tenant.

limitations: The announcement says GPT-5.5 is more token efficient than GPT-5.4, but APW records listed unit prices and does not estimate workload-level savings.
No raw announcement body is copied into APW.
